entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Important food legume cultivated in several countries

entities.name: MIDDLE EAST
entities.description: Origin area of chickpea between south-eastern Turkey and adjoining Syria

entities.name: TURKEY
entities.description: Country in the Middle East associated with the origin of chickpea

entities.name: SYRIA
entities.description: Country in the Middle East associated with the origin of chickpea

entities.name: EUROPEAN COUNTRIES
entities.description: Western region to which chickpea spread

entities.name: MYANMAR
entities.description: Eastern region to which chickpea spread

entities.name: CICEREAE
entities.description: Monogeneric tribe to which chickpea belongs

entities.name: FABACEAE
entities.description: Family to which chickpea belongs

entities.name: CICER
entities.description: Genus containing nine annuals and 34 perennial species

entities.name: CICER ARIETINUM
entities.description: The cultivated chickpea, a short annual herb

entities.name: GROWTH HABITS
entities.description: Plant growth forms ranging from prostrate to erect

entities.name: GLANDULAR AND NON-GLANDULAR HAIRS
entities.description: Plant coverings that secrete an acid mixture for defense

entities.name: SUCKING PESTS
entities.description: Pests that are defended against by the plant's hairs

entities.name: SELF-POLLINATION
entities.description: Process of pollination occurring due to anthers dehiscing before the flower opens

entities.name: DORMANCY
entities.description: Seed state of suspended growth, which is absent in chickpea

entities.name: DESI TYPE
entities.description: Chickpea variety characterized by dark-coloured seeds

entities.name: KABULI TYPE
entities.description: Chickpea variety characterized by beige-coloured seeds

entities.name: SOIL CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental factor affecting the time of germination

entities.name: MOISTURE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental factor affecting the time of germination

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of seed sprouting taking approximately one week
